topic: LangGraph TypeScript Guide: Build AI Agents in 2026

key_points: Comprehensive official-style guide updated April 2026 covering install, ReAct agents, streaming, persistence, and deployment
Recommends @langchain/langgraph + @langgraphjs/toolkit combo for production
Covers Node.js 20+ and TypeScript 5.4+ requirements
Includes comparison with CrewAI, OpenAI Agents SDK
Microsoft beginner-focused LangGraph.js guide with step-by-step walkthroughs
Focuses on core concepts: StateGraph, nodes, edges, conditional routing
Uses Zod schemas for TypeScript type safety in state definitions
Good for developers new to graph-based agent architectures
Practical multi-agent system construction with LangGraph in 2026
Covers supervisor/worker agent patterns and communication
Discusses real production deployments and pain points
Compares graph-based vs chain-based architectures for AI agents
Official LangChain repo: TypeScript agent notebooks including email, human-in-the-loop, and memory examples
Working code examples using latest API
Covers patterns: ReAct, tool-calling, interrupt/resume for HITL
Frequently referenced as canonical TypeScript implementation reference
Compares LangGraph, CrewAI, PydanticAI, Mastra, Vercel AI SDK for TypeScript teams
Mastra is TS-first and Vercel-native; better for serverless/Next.js stacks
LangGraph still leads for durable, stateful, graph-based agent workflows
Memory is identified as LangGraph biggest gap vs. Mastra/CrewAI

stats.fact: @langchain/langgraph TypeScript package sees over 42,000 weekly npm downloads as of April 2026
LangGraph TypeScript reached production stability in mid-2025, with feature parity to Python version (StateGraph, conditional edges, checkpointing, streaming, HITL)
Klarna uses LangGraph for a customer support bot serving 85 million active users
LangGraph powers production agent systems at Replit, Uber, LinkedIn, GitLab, and Elastic
Node.js 20 or later and TypeScript 5.4 or later are required for @langchain/langgraph
Four agentic orchestration frameworks dominate 2026 production: LangGraph (graph-based), CrewAI (role-based), Mastra (TypeScript-first), and OpenAI Agents SDK (provider-locked)
TypeScript teams switching from LangGraph to Mastra report setup time reduction from ~41 hours to ~18 hours for initial agent deployment
stats.source: DEV Community: I Switched from LangGraph to Mastra for My TypeScript Agents

angles: TypeScript-first guide: everything your team needs without touching Python docs
Full type safety advantage: how TypeScript catches entire bug categories at compile time that Python misses at runtime
Real production case studies: Klarna (85M users), Replit, LinkedIn — what they actually built
Streaming for real-time UX: why users expect live reasoning updates and how to implement them
Human-in-the-loop for high-stakes operations: pausing agents before financial transactions, emails, DB writes
LangGraph vs Mastra vs Vercel AI SDK: honest framework selection guide for TypeScript teams in 2026
Checkpointing and persistence: from MemorySaver to PostgreSQL without changing agent code

H2: Core Concepts: StateGraph, Nodes, and Edges Explained
H2: Building Your First ReAct Agent in TypeScript
H2: Adding Real-Time Streaming to Your LangGraph Agent
